Test Input/Condition: Run `streamlit run app.py`.
Expected Result: The application should start without errors.
Actual Result: Application started successfully at http://localhost:8501.

Test Input/Condition: Check for `fuel_prices.csv` in the project root.
Expected Result: The verified CSV file should exist.
Actual Result: The dataset file was found in the project root.

Test Input/Condition: Load the CSV and inspect column names.
Expected Result: Columns should match the required project schema.
Actual Result: Columns matched: Date, USD_KES, Crude_Oil, Super_Petrol, Diesel, Kerosene.

Test Input/Condition: Convert `Date` from text to datetime.
Expected Result: All dates should convert successfully.
Actual Result: All 52 records converted successfully to datetime values.

Test Input/Condition: Sort the dataset by `Date`.
Expected Result: Records should be in ascending month order.
Actual Result: Dataset sorted from Jan-2022 to Apr-2026.

Test Input/Condition: Create `Month_num` after sorting the dataset.
Expected Result: Month numbers should run sequentially.
Actual Result: Month_num was created from 1 to 52.

Test Input/Condition: Create lagged data for Super Petrol.
Expected Result: Lagged data should contain `Lag_1` and `Lag_2` and drop the first two rows.
Actual Result: Lagged Super Petrol dataset was created with 50 rows; first row is Mar-2022.

Test Input/Condition: Create lagged data for Diesel.
Expected Result: Diesel lagged data should contain `Lag_1` and `Lag_2`.
Actual Result: Lagged Diesel dataset was created successfully.

Test Input/Condition: Create lagged data for Kerosene.
Expected Result: Kerosene lagged data should contain `Lag_1` and `Lag_2`.
Actual Result: Lagged Kerosene dataset was created successfully.

Test Input/Condition: Train the linear regression model for Super Petrol.
Expected Result: The model should train and return evaluation metrics.
Actual Result: Model trained successfully. MAE=4.73, MSE=55.46, R?=-1.16.

Test Input/Condition: Train the linear regression model for Diesel.
Expected Result: The model should train and return evaluation metrics.
Actual Result: Model trained successfully. MAE=5.88, MSE=115.34, R?=-0.74.

Test Input/Condition: Train the linear regression model for Kerosene.
Expected Result: The model should train and return evaluation metrics.
Actual Result: Model trained successfully. MAE=2.16, MSE=9.75, R?=-5.52.

Test Input/Condition: Change the selected fuel type from Super Petrol to Diesel and Kerosene.
Expected Result: The page headings and prediction output should update to the selected fuel type.
Actual Result: The interface updated to show Diesel and Kerosene prediction headings correctly.

Test Input/Condition: Inspect the USD/KES widget in the app.
Expected Result: The widget should accept numeric values only.
Actual Result: The field rendered as a numeric spinbutton and accepted 129.24.

Test Input/Condition: Inspect the crude oil widget in the app.
Expected Result: The widget should accept numeric values only.
Actual Result: The field rendered as a numeric spinbutton and accepted 117.00.

Test Input/Condition: Use default values with Super Petrol selected.
Expected Result: The app should show next-month prediction in Kenya shillings.
Actual Result: The app displayed KSh 191.46 per litre for May 2026.

Test Input/Condition: Use default values with Diesel selected.
Expected Result: The app should show next-month prediction in Kenya shillings.
Actual Result: The app displayed KSh 184.96 per litre for May 2026.

Test Input/Condition: Use default values with Kerosene selected.
Expected Result: The app should show next-month prediction in Kenya shillings.
Actual Result: The app displayed KSh 154.00 per litre for May 2026.

Test Input/Condition: View the metric cards after prediction.
Expected Result: The app should display MAE, MSE, and R? Score.
Actual Result: All three metrics were displayed in the interface.

Test Input/Condition: Scroll to the trend chart area.
Expected Result: A line chart should display the selected fuel trend and predicted point.
Actual Result: The chart rendered successfully for the selected fuel.

Test Input/Condition: Open the historical dataset expander.
Expected Result: The verified dataset should be displayed in a table.
Actual Result: The expander opened and displayed historical fuel records.

Test Input/Condition: Open the lagged dataset expander.
Expected Result: The lagged dataset should be displayed with Lag_1 and Lag_2.
Actual Result: The expander opened and displayed lagged values for Super Petrol.

Test Input/Condition: Request the running local app URL once after startup.
Expected Result: The application should respond quickly in the browser.
Actual Result: The local response time measured about 250 ms.

Test Input/Condition: Open the app in Chromium through Playwright.
Expected Result: The interface should render correctly in a desktop browser.
Actual Result: The app rendered correctly in Chromium during screenshot capture.

Test Input/Condition: Inspect `app.py` feature columns.
Expected Result: Only Month_num, USD_KES, Crude_Oil, Lag_1, and Lag_2 should be used.
Actual Result: The implemented feature list matched the required lag-variable model.

Test Input/Condition: Check the running app against the stated project features.
Expected Result: The app should load data, predict next-month fuel price, show metrics, show chart, and show both dataset sections.
Actual Result: All required project features were confirmed in the live application.
